title: Warmly Spiced Peruvian Chicken Aji De Gallina
description: A malty, aerated, one-pot Peruvian dish featuring treacle and double-rise-bread, ovo-vegetarian-friendly, in the form of aji-de-gallina.
instructions: Heat the treacle in a large pot over medium heat until it starts to bubble.
Add the chopped aji amarillo pepper and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the heavy cream and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the chopped walnuts and cook for 1 minute.
Add the torn double-rise-bread and cook until the bread is toasted and the mixture is heated through.
Stir in the chopped hard-boiled egg and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the aji de gallina hot, garnished with additional chopped walnuts if desired.
